refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple requirements and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Establish real-time communication quality monitoring with feedback control to dynamically manage network resources
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. This supports functional independence and clear mapping from FR to DP.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate network condition data to inform adaptation
Risk that adaptation speed may not meet real-time requirements under rapidly changing network conditions
Potential complexity in protocol design to handle diverse network scenarios
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including error detection and correction as a complementary DP or as part of the adaptive protocol to enhance robustness against packet loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for handling communication protocols and network parameter adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network status data and send updated transmission parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network connection for monitoring and adjusting transmission parameters.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to network management; low diffusion as most related functions are within this module.
Vehicle module may use communication but does not primarily manage adaptive protocols, leading to moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication but does not implement protocol adaptation, causing high diffusion if contained here.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol management, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication parameters and can contain the logic with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If the adaptive protocol requires safety-related adjustments, coordination with Safety Services might increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, satisfying the acceptance criterion of packet loss ≤ 1%.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety-related adjustments might increase coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating that the adaptive communication protocol is well-contained within this module. Most related functions are centralized here, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces, which provide necessary data to confirm compliance with the packet loss acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution module is clear, as this module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for the adaptive protocol implementation.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to manage dependencies and minimize coupling.
Include safety-related communication adjustments in coordination protocols with Safety Services to ensure compliance with worksite safety plans.
Document monitoring and verification procedures for packet loss metrics within the NGHE Solution module to support traceability and validation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The adaptive communication protocol meets the acceptance criteria, and the NGHE Solution module shows good containment with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Recommended revisions pertain to interface contracts and documentation rather than decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before further decomposition to maintain clarity and control.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to manage dependencies and minimize coupling, and to document monitoring and verification procedures for packet loss metrics to support traceability and validation.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol as implemented in the NGHE Solution module fully satisfies the FR for packet loss ≤ 1%.
Interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can be clearly defined and managed to avoid excessive coupling.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from other modules may increase coupling if interface contracts are not well defined.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety-related adjustments may introduce complexity.
evidence_needed: Verification reports showing packet loss metrics compliance.
Safety coordination protocols and their validation.
